title: T-031 — Add ship.* permission strings to packages/rbac (11-shipping-k)
prompt:
# T-031 — Add ship.* permission strings to packages/rbac

PRD: docs/prd/11-SHIPPING-PRD.md §3 Personas & Roles, §6 D-SHP-7 RLS Multi-tenant, §6 D-SHP-8 SO Status Machine + Guards, §10 Quality Hold Integration (D-SHP-13), §13.1 SSCC-18, §14.4 BRCGS BOL retention
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Append the canonical `ship.*` permission group to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` so that every `<module>.<resource>.<verb>` capability declared by 11-SHIPPING is enum-locked before the rest of the module's Server Actions are written. ESLint enum-lock guard (02-settings T-046) blocks downstream usages otherwise.

## Implementation contract
1. Append the 14 ship.* permission strings (exact list below) to `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` following the existing `SETTINGS_CORE_PERMISSIONS` const-object pattern with JSDoc citing `docs/prd/11-SHIPPING-PRD.md §3` line numbers.
2. Export `ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS` as a typed `Permission[]` array literal so downstream Server Actions can iterate.
3. Each string MUST match regex `^ship\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` (3-segment dot-namespaced, lowercase + underscores only).
4. CODEOWNERS must keep `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` architect-locked.
5. Do NOT remove or rename existing permission strings, do NOT introduce a parallel 4-segment matrix (rejected per 02-settings PRD §3 [D2]).

## Permission strings (14)

- `ship.so.create` — create draft SO (shipping_manager) — §3, V-SHIP-SO-01
- `ship.so.confirm` — confirm SO (shipping_manager) — §3, §6 D-SHP-8, V-SHIP-SO-03
- `ship.so.cancel` — cancel SO (shipping_manager) — V-SHIP-SO-07
- `ship.hold.place` — place soft/credit/QA hold — §10.2, hold_place_modal
- `ship.hold.release` — release hold — hold_release_modal
- `ship.alloc.override` — override FEFO/expired/QA allocation w/ reason — §6 D-SHP-13 soft gate, V-SHIP-ALLOC-04, V-SHIP-ALLOC-05
- `ship.allergen.override` — QA override of allergen cascade conflict — V-SHIP-SO-03, allergen_overrides table
- `ship.pick.execute` — execute pick (scanner role) — §3, V-SHIP-PICK-01
- `ship.pack.close` — close pack carton, finalize SSCC — §13.1, V-SHIP-PACK-02
- `ship.ship.confirm` — confirm shipment (atomic outbox enqueue) — §6 D-SHP-14, V-SHIP-SHIP-01..04
- `ship.bol.sign` — upload signed BOL (BRCGS 7y retention) — §14.4, V-SHIP-LBL-04
- `ship.rma.disposition` — set RMA disposition (restock/scrap/quality_hold) — §8.5, V-SHIP-RMA-02..03
- `ship.dashboard.view` — view shipping dashboard — §15.1 SHIP-022
- `ship.dlq.replay` — replay shipping_push_dlq event (ops) — §12.6 DLQ ops screen

## Files
- packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts [modify]
- packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts [modify]
- CODEOWNERS [modify if missing]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the enum file is imported, when `ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS` is read, then it contains exactly the 14 strings listed above in declared order and no duplicates.
2. Given each permission key is parsed, when regex `^ship\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` is applied, then every key matches (vitest assertion).
3. Given vitest runs `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts`, when the suite executes, then it is green and asserts presence + regex + uniqueness + cross-module dedupe (no ship.* collides with foundation/settings/npd/wh/scanner/scheduler/production/quality strings).
4. Given CODEOWNERS is read, when grep is run, then `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` remains under `@architects`.

## Test strategy (RED first)
1. Extend `packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` with a `describe('ship.* permissions')` block asserting presence (14 strings), regex match, uniqueness, dedupe against existing `ALL_PERMISSIONS`.
2. RED: `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web vitest run packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts` — must fail.
3. GREEN: append const SHIP_PERMISSIONS = {...} and ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS array to permissions.enum.ts.
4. Re-run RED — must pass.
5. `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` in @monopilot/rbac must pass.

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce a 4-segment string (regex enforces 3 segments).
- Do not rename existing strings or move `ship.*` to a different prefix.
- Do not weaken or remove the existing CODEOWNERS architect lock on permissions.enum.ts.
- Do not commit ship.dlq.replay under any namespace other than `ship` (D365 dispatcher is module-owned per §12.6).
- Do not invent `ship.access` 2-segment role-shorthand (use 3-segment strings only).

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (RED→GREEN, typecheck, lint)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status

Note: the `ship.pack.close` permission gates SSCC label generation which reads `organizations.gs1_company_prefix` from the organizations table (02-settings) — always read from organizations table (02-settings), never from env config.

Note: `ship.bol.sign` and `ship.pod.upload` permissions gate BRCGS SHA-256 integrity hash operations on uploaded BOL/POD documents (7-year retention per BRCGS Issue 9 §3.5).

pipeline_inputs.description: Append 14 ship.* permission strings to packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts (RBAC foundation for §3 / §6 / §10 / §13.1 / §14.4).
pipeline_inputs.details: Use the same pattern as the existing SETTINGS_CORE_PERMISSIONS const-object: typed object with JSDoc citing PRD line numbers, then export ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS as a Permission[] literal. Strings must match `^ship\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`. Cross-module dedupe must be asserted (no overlap with ALL_FOUNDATION_PERMISSIONS, ALL_SETTINGS_CORE_PERMISSIONS, ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS, ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS, ALL_SCANNER_PERMISSIONS, ALL_SCHEDULER_PERMISSIONS, ALL_PRODUCTION_PERMISSIONS, ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS).
out_of_scope: Do not implement Server Actions or RBAC wrappers (downstream tasks)
Do not add ship.* permissions beyond the listed 14
Do not introduce a parallel 4-segment matrix
cross_module_dependencies: 09-quality/T-064 (consume gate — ship permissions include ship.hold.* which interface with quality hold gate per 2026-05-14 audit)
acceptance_criteria: Given the enum file is imported, when ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS is read, then it contains exactly the 14 listed strings, in order, with no duplicates.
Given each permission key is parsed, when regex `^ship\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` is applied, then every key matches.
Given vitest runs packages/rbac/src/__tests__/permissions.test.ts, when the suite executes, then presence + regex + uniqueness + cross-module dedupe all pass.
Given CODEOWNERS is read, when grep is run, then permissions.enum.ts remains under @architects.
test_strategy: RED: extend permissions.test.ts with describe('ship.* permissions') asserting the 14 strings + regex + uniqueness + dedupe; run vitest, expect failure.
GREEN: append SHIP_PERMISSIONS const-object and ALL_SHIP_PERMISSIONS typed array; re-run vitest.
Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac typecheck` and `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint` after change.
risk_red_lines: Do not introduce a 4-segment permission string.
Do not rename or remove existing permission strings.
Do not weaken the existing CODEOWNERS architect lock.
Do not invent 2-segment role-shorthand (use 3 segments only).
Do not commit ship.dlq.replay under any prefix other than ship.
Do not bypass the 09-quality consume gate (T-064 v_active_holds) — ship.hold.* permissions gate hold operations that interact with the quality hold system.
POD audit trail permissions (ship.bol.sign, ship.pod.upload) must be included in the enum — these gate BRCGS SHA-256 document hash operations.
